original: EntrepreneurElon MuskfoundsSpaceX
Hans Beck, German inventor of Playmobil toys, born in Greiz, Germany (d. 2009)

Irish organization repays a 170 year old favor, raising over $2 million (to date) for US Navajo Nation and Hopi Reservation badly affected by COVID-19. In 1840s Choctaw Nation sent $170 to aid Irish potato famine.
Baltimore OrioleCal Ripken Jrhits for cycle
Yuchiro Miura of Japan skis down Mt Everest

Exposition Universelle (World's Fair) in Paris opens with the recently completed Eiffel Tower serving as the entrance arch; the lifts in the tower are not ready, so intrepid visitors have to climb 1,710 steps to reach the top
Channel Tunnel linking Folkestone, England and Calais, France officially opens
US blacksmithJohn Deerecreates the first steel plough in Grand Detour, Illinois
World's first adhesive postage stamp, the "Penny Black", is first used in Great Britain
American physician and inventor John Gorrie patents a "refrigeration machine" to make ice

Samuel Pierpont Langley flies his unpiloted Number 5 aircraft using a catapult launch from a boat on the Potomac River, USA. The aircraft travels almost 3/4 of a mile - ten times further than any previous heavier-than-air flying machine.

Adriana Caselotti, American actress and singer, best known as the voice of Snow White in Walt Disney's animated film "Snow White and the Seven Dwarfs" (1937), born in Bridgeport, Connecticut (d. 1997)
One million plant and animal species are now at risk of extinction according to a major new UN report
Chaiten Volcano erupts in Chile, forcing the evacuation of more than 4,500 people
MLB Boston Red Sox score 12 runs in 4th inning, including record 4 consecutive triples hit by Carl Reynolds, Moose Solters,Rick Ferrell, and B Walters
Kerry Wood strikes out 20 Houston Astros to tie the major league record held byRoger Clemens. He threw a one-hitter and did not walk a batter in his 5th career start.
Chiaki Naito-Mukai, Japanese astronaut who took part is missions STS-65 and STS-95, born in Tatebayashi, Japan
Canadian province Alberta declares a state of emergency over unprecedented wildfires, more than 100, that have forced the evacuation of 25,000 people
17th NASA Space Shuttle Mission (51-B): Challenger 7 lands at Edwards AFB
Rubin "Hurricane" Carter, American boxer (famous murder conviction overturned after 19 years imprisonment), born in Clifton, New Jersey (d. 2014)
Linus Yale Jr.patents the Yale cylinder lock
The Rolling Stonesrelease the single "Paint It Black" in the US, featuring prominent sitar music played byBrian Jones
Bob Seger, American singer and songwriter ("Old Time Rock and Roll"; "Hollywood Nights"; "Against The Wind"), born in Detroit, Michigan
First international boxing match: Local fighter Bob Whittaker beats “The Venetian Gondolier”, Tito di Carni at James Figg's academy amphitheatre in Marylebone, London
Pablo Sandoval becomes second MLB player since 1900 to throw a scoreless outing, hit a home run and steal a base in the same game in SF Giants' 12-4 loss in Cincinnati; joinsChristy Mathewson1905
MLB St. Louis Browns Alva "Bobo" Holloman, in his first start game as starting pitcher, no-hits visiting Philadelphia A's, 6-0
Scotland Yard recovers stolen Johannes Vermeer oil painting "The Guitar Player" at  the cemetery of St Bartholomew-the-Great, in London's financial
France bans too thin fashion models and makes labeling of digitally enhanced photos mandatory
During a trip to Syria, PopeJohn Paul IIbecomes the first pope to enter a mosque
KingHenry VIIIorders a bible in English be placed in every church in England
Louis XIVof France moves his court to Versailles
Dutch colonist Peter Minuit organizes the purchase of Manhattan Island from Native Americans for 60 guilders worth of goods, believed to be the Canarsee Indians of the Lenape
Coronation of King Chares III and Queen Camilla at Westminster Abbey, London - first monarch crowned in the UK in 70 years
"Shrek the Third" directed by Chris Miller and Raman Hui with voices byMike Myers,Cameron DiazandEddie Murphypremieres in Westwood Ca.
Gaston Leroux, French writer (The Phantom of the Opera), born in Paris (d. 1927)
Columbia Records releases "Johnny Cashat Folsom Prison", the first live album by American singer-songwriter Johnny Cash, recorded in January 1968
British-Canadian boxerLennox Lewis(28) TKOs American Phil Jackson (29) in 8th round for WBC heavyweight title at Convention Hall in Atlantic City, New Jersey
Nelson Mandelaand the ANC, finally confirmed winners in South Africa's first post apartheid election
US Civil War: Union ArmyGeneral Shermanbegins advance to Atlanta, Georgia, during the Atlanta Campaign
Battle of Edington:Alfred the Greatand his West Saxon army defeat Viking army of Guthrum the Old [earliest possible date]
Space Shuttle STS 39 (Discovery 12) lands
STS-55 (Columbia) lands
Donald A Thomas, PhD/Astronaut (STS 65, 70, 83, 94), born in Cleveland, Ohio
Ronald Rivest, American computer scientist (RSA cryptography), born in Schenectady, New York[1]
John P. Allen, American engineer, inventor and CEO (Biosphere 2), born in Carnegie, Oklahoma
David Leestma, American astronaut (STS 41-G, 28, 45), born in Muskegon, Michigan
John Flansburgh, American alt-rock singer-songwriter, and guitarist (They Might Be Giants - "Istanbul (Not Constantinople)"; "Birdhouse Of Your Soul"), born in Lincoln, Massachusetts
James Turrell, American artist (Roden crater), born in Los Angeles, California
Maureen Wilton runs female world record marathon (3:15:22)
Early warnings provided by REACT (ham radio operators) means only
An earthquake strikes Friuli in Northern Italy, causes 989 deaths and the destruction of entire villages
Donald E. Pelotte becomes the first Native American Catholic bishop
US PresidentEisenhowersigns Civil Rights Act of 1960
Epping Forest, England, dedicated byQueen Victoria
Battle at Gogra: Mughal EmperorBaburbeats Afghans and Bengals
Sejong the Great, 4th King of Joseon (1418-50), born in Hanseong, Kingdom of Joseon (d. 1450)
Martin Delany, African-American abolitionist and physician (1st African American major in US Army), born in Charles Town, Virginia (d. 1885)
Joseph Brackett, American religious leader (Shakers) and composer (Simple Gifts), born in Cumberland, Maine (d. 1882)
Phoebe Ann Coffin Hanaford, 1st female ordained minister in New England, born in Siasconset, Massachusetts (d. 1921)
Willem de Sitter, Dutch scientist and cosmologist (developed theoretical models of the universe based on Albert Einstein’s general theory of relativity), born in Sneek, Netherlands (d. 1934)
